name: Mu Shu Vegetables
recipeIngredient: 1 package mu shu pancakes (check these for fat content first)
a few wood ear or cloud ear mushrooms, soaked in hot water for 30 minutes and then cut into thin strips
1 large onion, cut into very thin wedges
1 small head green cabbage, finely shredded
4 oz can mushrooms, drained (or equivalent fresh, sauteed in water)
3-4 green onions, cut into 2 inch pieces
Soy sauce or tamari to taste
recipeInstructions:
Thaw the mu shu pancakes.
In a large wok or skillet, sauté the garlic and onions for three minutes in a little water or vegetable broth. Add the cabbage, carrot, and mushrooms and sauté until the cabbage just wilts. Add the green onions, soy sauce (about 1-2 tbsp is good), and about 1 tbsp of hosin sauce. Heat through.
Place one pancake in the microwave for 10 seconds to make them more pliable. Remove and spread with a tablespoon of hosin sauce, then about 1/2 cup of the mu shu mixture. Fold the bottom of the pancake up about 1/4 of the way, then fold the two sides in. Roll them up so they are easier to eat.
You can add julienne strips of tofu or other vegetables (such as bean sprouts, celery, water chestnuts, bamboo shoots) if desired. This dish is even better the second day; do not put the ingredients in the pancakes until right before eating.
